218	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section surjectivity for the affine cover system -/
219	
220	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
221	/-- **Section surjectivity for the affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing` field;
222	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`). Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be a short exact sequence of
223	`O_X`-modules on `Spec R` whose left term `S₁` has vanishing positive Čech cohomology over every
224	standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (`hvanish`). Then the section map `S₂(D f) → S₃(D f)` is surjective over
225	every distinguished open `D f`. This discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`.
226	
227	Proof: `S.g` is an epi of `O_X`-modules, so (via `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` + the sheaf
228	local-surjectivity criterion) the underlying map of abelian presheaves is locally surjective. A
229	section `t ∈ S₃(D f)` therefore lifts locally on an open cover of `D f`; refine it to a standard
230	cover `D(gᵢ)` (`standard_cover_cofinal`) carrying local lifts, feed the cover, the lifts and the
231	vanishing `Ȟ¹(𝒰, S₁) = 0` to the Čech `Ȟ¹`-surjectivity criterion `ses_cech_h1`, and glue to a
232	global lift. Project-local: the affine instantiation of the `ses_cech_h1` criterion. -/
233	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
234	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
235	    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f' : R),
236	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f' : (Spec R).Opens)
237	        = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
238	      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
239	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
240	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
241	    (f : R) :
242	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
243	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
244	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
245	          (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) := by
246	  classical
247	  set V₀ : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hV₀
248	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
249	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
250	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
251	  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
252	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
253	  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
254	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
255	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
256	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
257	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
258	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
259	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
260	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
261	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
262	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
263	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
264	  intro t
265	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
266	  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
267	      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
268	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
269	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
270	    intro p
271	    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
272	    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
273	  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
274	  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
275	    intro x hx
276	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
277	  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)`.
278	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal f W hVcov
279	  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
280	  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
281	    rw [hU, hV₀, hVeq]
282	    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
283	  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
284	  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
285	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
286	      (sLift (φ i.down)) with hsLoc
287	  -- the section `t` transported to `⨆ U` (`= V₀`)
288	  have hopV : (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R))ᵒᵖ) = Opposite.op V₀ :=
289	    congrArg Opposite.op hUsup
290	  set s : ToType (HX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i))) :=
291	    ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t with hs
292	  -- assemble the hypotheses of `ses_cech_h1`
293	  have hπι : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (FX.presheaf.obj V)),
294	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0 := by
295	    intro V x
296	    have hz : fι ≫ gπ = 0 := by
297	      rw [hfι, hgπ, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, S.zero,
298	        Functor.map_zero, Functor.map_zero]
299	    have := congrArg (fun (ψ : FX.presheaf ⟶ HX.presheaf) => ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app V) x) hz
300	    simpa using this
301	  have hmono : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ),
302	      Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)) := by
303	    intro V
304	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
305	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
306	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
307	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
308	    haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
309	    have hmonoAb : Mono (fι.app V) :=
310	      inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).map S.f))
311	    rwa [AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective] at hmonoAb
312	  have hker : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj V)),
313	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x := by
314	    intro V x hx
315	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
316	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
317	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
318	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
319	    have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V))).Exact :=
320	      ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
321	        (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) hS.mono_f inferInstance
322	    rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hex
323	    exact (hex x).mp hx
324	  -- the vanishing input, packaged as homology of the section Čech complex
325	  have hcovf : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
326	      = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) := by
327	    rw [hVeq]
328	    exact (Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))).symm
329	  have hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U FX).homology 1) := hvanish n g f hcovf 1 one_pos
330	  -- the local-lift compatibility `gπ(sLocᵢ) = s |_ Uᵢ`
331	  have hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op (U i))) (sLoc i)
332	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op) s := by
333	    intro i
334	    simp only [hsLoc, hs]
335	    erw [gπ.naturality_apply (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op (sLift (φ i.down)), hsLift (φ i.down)]
336	    show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
337	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op) t)
338	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op)
339	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t)
340	    have hmaps : HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op
341	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op
342	        = HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)
343	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op := by
344	      rw [← HX.presheaf.map_comp, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
345	      congr 1
346	    simp only [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
347	    rw [← hmaps]
348	    exact (ConcreteCategory.comp_apply _ _ t).symm
349	  -- apply the Čech surjectivity criterion
350	  obtain ⟨glob, hglob⟩ := ses_cech_h1 U FX GX HX fι gπ S.X₂.isSheaf S.X₃.isSheaf
351	    hπι hmono hker s hH1 sLoc hlift
352	  -- transport the global lift back to `V₀`
353	  refine ⟨ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV)) glob, ?_⟩
354	  erw [gπ.naturality_apply (eqToHom hopV) glob, hglob]
355	  show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV))
356	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t) = t
357	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
358	  simp only [eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
359	  rfl

361	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the affine cover system (Stacks 02KG) -/
362	
363	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
364	/-- **The affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `def:affine_cover_system`). For `X = Spec R`, the
365	basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D f` (`f : R`) and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the
366	standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (indexed by `ULift (Fin n)`, so as to land in `Type u`). The
367	three nontrivial fields are discharged by:
368	- `faces_mem` ← `affine_faces_mem` (finite intersections of distinguished opens are distinguished);
369	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (the `ses_cech_h1` section-surjectivity criterion);
370	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity, applied
371	  directly to the distinguished opens of each standard cover — no `Spec R_f` restriction detour).
372	Project-local: the affine instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`. -/
373	noncomputable def affineCoverSystem (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
374	  B := Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens))
375	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
376	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
377	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
378	      = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) }
379	  faces_mem := by
380	    rintro c ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
381	    exact affine_faces_mem (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
382	  surj_of_vanishing := by
383	    rintro S hSE hvanish V ⟨f, rfl⟩
384	    refine affine_surj_of_vanishing S hSE (fun n g f' hcov q hq => ?_) f
385	    exact hvanish ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩
386	      ⟨n, g, f', rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
387	  injective_acyclic := by
388	    rintro I hI c ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
389	    haveI : Injective I := hI
390	    have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam
391	      (U := fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) I q hq
392	    exact hfam
393	
394	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change of the cover spanning condition -/
395	
396	/-- **A standard cover of `D(f)` spans the unit ideal of `R_f`.** If the distinguished opens
397	`D(gᵢ)` cover `D(f)` in `Spec R`, then the images `gᵢ ↦ R_f` of the cover family span the whole
398	ring `R_f = Localization.Away f`: pulled back along `Spec R_f ≅ D(f) ↪ Spec R` the union of the
399	`D(gᵢ)` becomes `D(f)` itself, which is all of `Spec R_f` since `f` is a unit there. Project-local:
400	the spanning hypothesis required to apply the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing
401	`sectionCech_affine_vanishing` over `R_f` in the quasi-coherent seed's change-of-base leaf. -/
402	theorem affine_cover_span_localizationAway {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
403	    (g : ι → R) (f : R)
404	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
405	      = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i)) :
406	    Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (g i))) = ⊤ := by
407	  rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff]
408	  simp only [← PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen]
409	  rw [← map_iSup, ← hcov, PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen, eq_top_iff]
410	  rintro p -
411	  rw [PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen]
412	  exact fun hmem => p.isPrime.ne_top
413	    (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit f))
414	
415	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech cohomology transports along isomorphisms -/
416	
417	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficient presheaves.** An
418	isomorphism `e : F ≅ G` of presheaves of `𝒪_X`-modules induces an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech
419	complexes (`sectionCechComplexFunctor`), hence of their degree-`p` homologies; so vanishing of
420	`Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` transfers to `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, G)`. Project-local: the naturality used to reduce the
421	quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case via `F ≅ ~(ΓF)`. -/
422	theorem cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
423	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ≅ G) (p : ℕ)
424	    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
425	  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ℕ) p).mapIso
426	    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm
427	
428	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed (reduction) -/
429	
430	/-- **Reduction of the quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case.**  For a
431	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the affine structure theorem `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
432	gives `F ≅ ~M` with `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`; transporting along this isomorphism
433	(`cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F`
434	to the tilde-case standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing supplied in `htilde`.  Project-local: isolates
435	the single remaining geometric obligation (positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing of `~M` over a
436	standard cover of a distinguished open `D(f)`), which is the change-of-base-to-`R_f` leaf. -/
437	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
438	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
439	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
440	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
441	          = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
442	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
443	          IsZero (cechCohomology
444	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
445	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
446	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
447	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F := by
448	  intro c hc p hp
449	  obtain ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
450	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
451	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
452	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
453	  exact htilde n g f hcov p hp

455	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Serre vanishing on affines (assembly reduction) -/
456	
457	/-- **Serre vanishing on affines, reduced to the tilde {\v C}ech-vanishing leaf** (Stacks 02KG).
458	Instantiates the basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) at the affine
459	cover system `affineCoverSystem R`, taking the whole affine `⊤ = D(1)` for the basic open: the
460	absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(Spec R, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU ⊤, F)` of a quasi-coherent `F` vanishes for
461	`p > 0`, modulo the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing `htilde`.  Carries
462	`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]` exactly as `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` does.  Project-local:
463	verifies the full Lane-1 assembly end-to-end, so that the blueprint targets `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`
464	and `affine_serre_vanishing` both reduce to the single residual `htilde` (the change-of-base-to-`R_f`
465	leaf). -/
466	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
467	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
468	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
469	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
470	          = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
471	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
472	          IsZero (cechCohomology
473	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
474	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
475	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
476	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
477	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)) F p) : e = 0 :=
478	  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystem R)
479	    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing F htilde)
480	    ⊤ ⟨1, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one⟩ p hp e
481	
482	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the unconditional 02KG tops -/
483	
484	/-- **The standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing residual `htilde`, discharged unconditionally.**  For a
485	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F` with global module `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`, the positive-degree
486	section {\v C}ech cohomology of the tilde sheaf `~M` over any standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` of a

518	discharging the `htilde` hypothesis of `affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing` with the

551	open* `V` of `Spec R`.  Given an arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of `V`, there is a

553	`D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  The only change from `standard_cover_cofinal` is the source of

555	refinement step for `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` over a general affine open. -/
556	theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
557	    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {α : Type u} (W : α → (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
558	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
559	      V = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
560	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
561	  classical
562	  have hK : IsCompact (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) := hV.isCompact
563	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
564	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
565	  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
566	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `V ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
567	  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ V ⊓ W p.2}
568	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
569	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
570	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `V`.
571	  have hsub : (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
572	    intro x hx
573	    have hxV : x ∈ V := hx
574	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxV)
575	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((V ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
576	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
577	      exact ⟨hxV, hxa⟩
578	    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
579	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (V ⊓ W a).isOpen
580	    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
581	    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
582	    have hle : B r ≤ V ⊓ W a := by
583	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
584	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
585	    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
586	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
587	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
588	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
589	  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
590	  let e := t.equivFin

593	    · intro x hx
594	      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
595	      refine TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
596	      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
597	      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
598	    · exact iSup_le fun i => le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
599	  · intro i
600	    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right

744	-- The structure literal forces three nontrivial field elaborations (`faces_mem`,
745	-- `surj_of_vanishing`, `injective_acyclic`), each unfolding large presheaf data.
746	/-- **The enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG), with basis `B` the *all affine opens* of
747	`Spec R` (rather than only the distinguished `D f` of `affineCoverSystem`) and admissible coverings
748	`Cov` the standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine.  The three fields:
749	- `faces_mem` ← faces `⨅ₖ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished, hence affine
750	  (`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`);
751	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (general-affine-open surjectivity);
752	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity).
753	Project-local: the enlarged instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by
754	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` to obtain general-affine-open Serre vanishing. -/
755	noncomputable def affineCoverSystemGeneral (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
756	  B := { U : (Spec R).Opens | IsAffineOpen U }
757	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
758	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
759	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
760	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) }
761	  faces_mem := by
762	    rintro c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
763	    have h := basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
764	    exact h ▸ isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen
765	      (∏ k, (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) (σ k))

767	    rintro S hSE hvanish V hV
768	    refine affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine S hSE (fun n g haff q hq => ?_) V hV
769	    exact hvanish ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩
770	      ⟨n, g, rfl, haff⟩ q hq
771	  injective_acyclic := by
772	    rintro I hI c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
773	    haveI : Injective I := hI
774	    have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam
775	      (U := fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) I q hq

778	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — general-affine-open Serre vanishing (reduced to the seed)
779	
780	The **residual** of the enlarged route is the positive-degree quasi-coherent Čech-vanishing *seed*
781	`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` for `p > 0` over
782	every standard cover `𝒰 : i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine* (not just a distinguished
783	`D(f)`). Unlike the distinguished case — discharged by
784	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` via change of base to a single
785	`Rf = Localization.Away f` — a general affine open `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` has no single `f` with `V = D(f)`,
786	so the seed needs a change of base to `Γ(V, 𝒪)` (equivalently, the identification of the section
787	Čech complex over `{D(gᵢ) ⊆ Spec R}` with the standard-cover complex of `~(Γ(V,F))` over
788	`Spec Γ(V)` under `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)`). That change-of-scheme identification is the lone remaining
789	geometric leaf; it is isolated here as the explicit hypothesis `hseed`. -/
790	
791	/-- **Reduction of the general-affine-open quasi-coherent seed to the tilde case.**  For a
792	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, transporting along `F ≅ ~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`,
793	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` to the
794	tilde-case positive-degree Čech vanishing `htilde`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, ~M)` over every standard
795	cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union is affine.  Verbatim analogue of
796	`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` for the enlarged cover system; isolates the single
797	geometric residual (the change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` tilde-section identification).  Project-local: the
798	cleanest statement of the general-affine-open seed residual. -/
799	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
800	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
801	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
802	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
803	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
804	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
805	          IsZero (cechCohomology
806	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
807	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
808	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
809	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F := by
810	  intro c hc p hp
811	  obtain ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
812	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
813	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
814	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
815	  exact htilde n g hcov p hp
816	
817	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the Čech-vanishing seed** (Stacks 02KG,
818	generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
819	and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
820	vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the
821	basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged cover system
822	`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target
823	of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
824	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
825	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
826	    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
827	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
828	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
829	  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) hseed V hV p hp e
830	
831	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the tilde Čech-vanishing leaf**
832	(Stacks 02KG, generalised).  Composes `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (seed
833	from tilde vanishing) with `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed`: for quasi-coherent `F` and any
834	affine open `V`, `Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F) = 0` for `p > 0`, modulo the tilde-section change-of-base
835	leaf `htilde`.  Project-local: the fully-reduced Need #2 top, leaving exactly the change-of-scheme
836	tilde residual. -/
837	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
838	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
839	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
840	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
841	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
842	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
843	          IsZero (cechCohomology
844	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
845	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
846	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
847	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
848	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
849	  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed F
850	    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing F htilde) V hV p hp e
851	
852	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the unconditional general-affine 02KG tops (Need #2) -/
853	
854	/-- **The general-affine standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing residual, discharged unconditionally.**
855	The general-affine analogue of `affine_tildeVanishing`: for a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
856	with global module `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`, the positive-degree section {\v C}ech cohomology of the tilde
857	sheaf `~M` over any standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union is an affine open* vanishes. Proved by
858	the now-built change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` theorem `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (route B1,
859	base change to `S = Γ(V)`), with `s := g ∘ down`, replacing the single-`R_f` change of base of
860	`affine_tildeVanishing`. Project-local: bundles the residual leaf in the precise shape the general
861	`_of_tildeVanishing` forms consume. -/
862	private theorem affine_tildeVanishing_general {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (F : (Spec R).Modules)
863	    (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R)
864	    (haff : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
865	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)))
866	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p) :
867	    IsZero (cechCohomology
868	      (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
869	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
870	        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p) :=
871	  sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F)
872	    (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g i.down) haff p hp
873	
874	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, unconditional** (Stacks 02KG, generalised from
875	`D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open; closes Need #2). For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
876	and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
877	vanishes for `p > 0`. Obtained by discharging the tilde-{\v C}ech-vanishing leaf `htilde` of
878	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing` with the now-proved residual
879	`affine_tildeVanishing_general` (in turn `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen`). Project-local:
880	the general-affine 02KG top, discharging the Need #2 general-affine cone end-to-end. -/
881	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_open {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
882	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
883	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
884	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
885	  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing F
886	    (affine_tildeVanishing_general F) V hV p hp e
887	
888	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImagePresheaf
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AffineSerreVanishing
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections
11	
12	/-!
13	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
14	
15	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
16	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
17	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
18	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
19	
20	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
21	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
22	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
23	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports
24	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean`.  Placing `cechAugmented_exact` in this downstream file
25	(`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`) makes all those ingredients available without a cycle.
26	The pure-Mathlib site lemmas (`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`,
27	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`, `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`)
28	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.
29	
30	Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
31	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
32	  `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
33	-/
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	open Scheme.Modules
42	
43	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
44	
45	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
46	
47	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
48	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
49	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
50	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
51	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
52	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
53	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
54	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
55	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
56	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
57	  apply F.map_injective
58	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
59	  exact h
60	
61	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
62	
63	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
64	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
65	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
66	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
67	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
68	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
69	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
70	
71	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
72	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
73	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
74	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
75	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
76	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
77	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
78	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
79	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
80	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
81	
82	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
83	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
84	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
85	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
86	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
87	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
88	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
89	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
90	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
91	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
92	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
93	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
94	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)
95	
96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/
97	
98	open Opposite in
99	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
100	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
101	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
102	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
103	
104	This packages the three imported site lemmas
105	(`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`) into the "locally zero" form the
106	{\v C}ech-resolution argument consumes: the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)` is a zero
107	object over every `V` contained in a cover member, and those `V` are a covering basis.
108	The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero presheaf) comes from the
109	hypothesis (each member's section group is a subsingleton); the local surjectivity is
110	free (the target is a zero object). Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
111	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C]
112	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
113	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
114	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
115	    (h : ∀ U : C, ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
116	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :
117	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
118	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
119	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
120	    intro x
121	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
122	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
123	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
124	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
125	    refine ⟨?_⟩
126	    intro X x y _
127	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop
128	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
129	    intro V g hg
130	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
131	    haveI := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSzero hg)
132	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
133	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
134	    refine ⟨?_⟩
135	    intro U s
136	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
137	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
138	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
139	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
140	  exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective
141	    J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
142	
143	/- Planner strategy:
144	Route: sections + sheafification (NOT stalks — `SheafOfModules.stalk` is absent from Mathlib).
145	Step 1: reflect `IsZero (homology p)` through the faithful additive forgetful functor
146	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (it preserves zero morphisms, so it reflects the zero object). Step 2:
147	the homology SHEAF = sheafification of the PRESHEAF homology, via the project engine
148	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean). Step 3: the presheaf
149	homology is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`, locally zero on the affine basis — over each basic affine `D(g) ⊆ Uᵢ`,
150	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` gives `F|_{D(g)} ≅ ~M` and
151	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (CechAcyclic.lean) kills positive-degree homology;
152	the basic affines are cofinal (`standard_cover_cofinal` / `affineCoverSystem`), so the map
153	`0 → presheaf-homology` is locally bijective, hence its sheafification (= the homology sheaf) is
154	zero. The reusable abelian-sheaf site lemmas `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W` /`_of_W_isZero`/
155	`_of_isLocallyBijective` (in CechHigherDirectImage.lean, importable) discharge the site-theory half.
156	The ONE bridge to build here: connect the module-level `homologyIsoSheafify` to those abelian-sheaf
157	site lemmas via the sheafification square `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ forget`
158	(cf. `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`, used in AffineSerreVanishing.lean; see
159	`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md` and the iter-053 mathlib-analogist report on the toSheaf-reflection
160	bridge). Diamond-prone — work with `.hom` not `.val`, defeq/`change` not `rw`. Step 4: the
161	degree-0 augmentation node uses the same spanning-family exactness (`exact_of_isLocalized_span` /
162	`combDifferential_exact`). Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
163	-/
164	
165	/-- **The augmented Čech complex is a resolution** (Stacks
166	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`; blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`).
167	
168	Let `X` be a separated scheme, `𝒰` a finite open cover of `X` all of whose members are
169	affine (so every finite intersection `U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₚ}` is affine by separatedness),
170	and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. Then every homology object of the augmented Čech
171	complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is zero:
172	```
173	  ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p).
174	```
175	Equivalently, the {\v C}ech nerve of `𝒰` is a resolution of `F` in `X.Modules`. -/
176	theorem cechAugmented_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
177	    (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) [X.IsSeparated]
178	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
179	    ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
180	  intro p
181	  -- Step 1: reflect through the faithful, zero-preserving `toSheaf`.
182	  apply isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
183	  -- Step 2: transport `IsZero` across `homologyIsoSheafify` and the sheafification square.
184	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
185	  set cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ with hcc
186	  set K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F with hK
187	  set P := ((((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
188	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K).homology p) with hP
189	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
190	    ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
191	        (PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify α cc K p) ≪≫
192	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
193	  -- Step 3: the presheaf homology is locally a zero object on the cover-member basis.
194	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
195	  apply isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
196	  -- Section-level vanishing on every open `V` contained in a cover member `Uᵢ`:
197	  -- the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the member `Uᵢ ∩ V = V`, giving a contracting
198	  -- (prepend-`i_fix`) homotopy on the augmented section complex over `V`. (F-agnostic.)
199	  have hSec : ∀ (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀),
200	      V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i → IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
201	    intro V i hiV
202	    -- Evaluate-at-`V` the underlying presheaf complex: this functor is additive and
203	    -- preserves homology, so `Q.obj (op V) ≅ Hᵖ(Γ(V, K•))` (the section complex homology).
204	    set GV := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
205	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V) with hGV
206	    set Kp := (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
207	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K) with hKp
208	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GV.mapHomologyIso' cc Kp p).symm
209	    -- Remaining: the augmented section complex `Γ(V, cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F)` is exact
210	    -- in degree `p`.  Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the
211	    -- member `coverOpen 𝒰 i ∩ V = V`; prepending that fixed index `i` is a contracting
212	    -- homotopy `d∘h + h∘d = id` on the section complex (template
213	    -- `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` / the objectwise homotopy of FreePresheafComplex),
214	    -- so every homology object vanishes.  F-agnostic, cover-agnostic.
215	    -- Step 3(d): the residual `Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`.  The intended discharge consumes the
216	    -- Sub-brick A chain in `CechSectionIdentification.lean` — `cechSection_complex_iso`
217	    -- (`D ≅ D'`, the per-degree section identification) and `cechSection_contractible`
218	    -- (`Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0`, the prepend-`i` contracting homotopy on the concrete section
219	    -- {\v C}ech complex `D'`, valid since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`) — combined by the consumer

259	hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
260	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
261	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
262	cosimplicial naturality.
263	
264	Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
265	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
266	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
267	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
268	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
269	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)
270	
271	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — hom-complex as contravariant transport
272	
273	The injective-acyclicity assembly (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, gated on Lane-1's
274	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`) needs `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` — the alternating *coface* complex of
275	the hom-cosimplicial object `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` — to be identified with the
276	contravariant transport `Hom(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` of the *opposite* of the free
277	Čech chain complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Once this identification is in hand,
278	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (applied to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op`) and
279	`cechComplex_hom_identification` combine in a single step to turn the free resolution into
280	Čech acyclicity of injective sheaves.
281	
282	Project-local: the comparison of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped opposite
283	of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
284	
285	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
286	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Holds definitionally (both unfold to
287	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).map (SimplexCategory.δ i).op).op`);
288	isolated as a `rfl` lemma to drive the differential identification `homCechComplex_d_eq`. -/
289	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
290	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ℕ} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
291	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).δ i
292	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i).op :=
293	  rfl
294	
295	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
296	the free Čech complex.  Both differentials are alternating sums of (co)faces; pushing the
297	opposite and the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` through the alternating sum on the right and
298	using `homCechCosimplicial_δ` matches them term by term.  This is the naturality input for the
299	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
300	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
301	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
302	    (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
303	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
304	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))).d p (p + 1) := by
305	  have hL : (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F) p :=
307	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
308	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p
309	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) p :=
310	    ChainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
311	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
312	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
313	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
314	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
315	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
316	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
317	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
318	    (fun i => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i)
319	  erw [hop, Functor.map_sum]
320	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
321	  rw [homCechCosimplicial_δ, CategoryTheory.op_zsmul]
322	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
323	  rfl
324	
325	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
326	
327	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` identifying the
328	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the contravariant transport
329	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free Čech chain
330	complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Degreewise the two complexes have the *same* term
331	(`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`), so the components are identities; the differential squares are
332	`homCechComplex_d_eq`.
333	
334	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into Čech acyclicity: applying
335	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op` produces a
336	quasi-isomorphism of mapped-opposite complexes, and this iso transports it onto
337	`homCechComplex 𝒰 F` (hence, via `cechComplex_hom_identification`, onto `sectionCechComplex`).
338	
339	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
340	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
341	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
342	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
343	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅
344	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
345	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰)) :=
346	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
347	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
348	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans
349	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq 𝒰 F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))
350	
351	/-- **Section Čech complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
352	
353	Composing `homCechComplexMapOpIso` with the Čech hom-identification
354	`cechComplex_hom_identification` gives a cochain-complex isomorphism between the mapped
355	opposite of the free Čech complex and the section Čech complex `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)`.  This is
356	the single isomorphism needed for the injective-acyclicity assembly: once Lane 1 provides
357	`QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`, mapping its opposite through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`
358	(quasi-iso by `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`) and transporting the source/target
359	across this iso identifies `sectionCechComplex` with the mapped opposite of a complex
360	concentrated in degree `0`, giving positive-degree vanishing.
361	
362	Project-local: assembled from two project-local isomorphisms. -/
363	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
364	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
365	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
366	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))
367	      ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
368	  (homCechComplexMapOpIso 𝒰 F).symm ≪≫ cechComplex_hom_identification 𝒰 F
369	
370	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Hom(-, I)` is exact for injective `I`
371	
372	The presheaf-level Čech-acyclicity argument (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`) needs the single
373	categorical fact that, for an injective object `I` of an abelian category, the contravariant
374	hom functor `Hom(-, I)` is exact — equivalently, the covariant functor
375	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` preserves homology. Mathlib supplies the two pieces:
376	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` always preserves finite limits (it is left exact), and injectivity
377	is equivalent to it preserving epimorphisms; we assemble these into preservation of finite
378	colimits (hence of homology), and feed the result through
379	`HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` to obtain quasi-isomorphism
380	preservation. These are stated for a general abelian category so they apply verbatim to
381	`X.PresheafOfModules` once the injectivity of `I` as a presheaf of modules is in hand.
382	
383	Project-local: Mathlib has the ingredients but not the packaged exactness of `Hom(-, I)` as a
384	homology-preserving functor. -/
385	
386	section InjectiveHomExact
387	
388	open CategoryTheory Limits
389	
390	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
391	
392	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
393	of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`
394	preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
395	injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
396	exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
397	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.
398	
399	Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
400	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
401	instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
402	    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
403	  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
404	    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
405	  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
406	  intro S hS
407	  have hg := hS.epi_g
408	  exact ⟨hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
409	    hepi.preserves S.g⟩
410	
411	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
412	Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
413	(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
414	quasi-isomorphism `φ` of chain complexes in `Cᵒᵖ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
415	`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
416	augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
417	and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.
418	
419	Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
420	exactness instance above. -/
421	lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
422	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex Cᵒᵖ c}
423	    (φ : K ⟶ L) [QuasiIso φ] :
424	    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  exact HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology φ (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) i
428	    (hφ := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt (f := φ) i)
429	
430	end InjectiveHomExact
431	
432	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech `H¹` vanishing ⟹ cocycles are coboundaries
433	
434	The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
435	`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
436	`Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
437	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
438	statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
439	local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
440	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
441	coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
442	
443	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
444	neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/
445	
446	section CechH1Coboundary
447	
448	open AlgebraicTopology
449	
450	/-- **Homology vanishing ⟹ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
451	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
452	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
453	consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
454	homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
455	`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.
456	
457	Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
458	`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
459	theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
460	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
461	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
462	    Function.Exact
463	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
464	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
465	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
466	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
467	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
468	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
469	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
470	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
471	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
472	  have key : Function.Exact
473	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
474	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
475	    have hh := h
476	    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
477	        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
478	        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
479	    exact hh
480	  rw [hf, hg] at key
481	  exact key
482	
483	/-- **Čech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0`** — the
484	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.
485	
486	Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
487	`c σ ∈ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` indexed by double-indices `σ : Fin 2 → ι`), if the family
488	satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
489	over each triple-index `σ : Fin 3 → ι` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
490	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
491	`t` (sections `t σ ∈ F(U (σ 0))` over single-indices) with `c σ` the alternating sum
492	of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c σ = t_{σ 1}|_{σ} - t_{σ 0}|_{σ}` once the
493	two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
494	{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
495	
496	Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
497	complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
498	theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
499	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
500	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
501	    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
502	    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
503	      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
504	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
505	          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
506	    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
507	      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
508	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
509	          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
510	  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
511	  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
512	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
513	  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
514	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
515	  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
516	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
517	    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
518	    funext σ
519	    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
520	    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
521	      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
522	    exact hcoc σ
523	  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
524	  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
525	  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]
526	
527	end CechH1Coboundary
528	
529	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹`
530	vanishing (`ses_cech_h1`)
531	
532	This section assembles the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing into the surjectivity statement
533	`ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).  The
534	{\v C}ech-algebra heart is `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` (the
535	cocycle-to-coboundary extraction proven above); the remaining content is the standard
536	sheaf theory — local lifting, left-exactness on sections, and gluing — which here is
537	supplied through Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and a handful of
538	restriction-bookkeeping helpers.
539	
540	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
541	the surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is new here. -/
542	
543	section SesCechH1
544	
545	open AlgebraicTopology TopologicalSpace Opposite
546	
547	/-- **Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction.**
548	For `A ≤ B ≤ C` the restriction `P(C) → P(B)` followed by `P(B) → P(A)` equals the direct
549	restriction `P(C) → P(A)`.  Holds definitionally for `Ab`-valued presheaves on opens
550	because the composite of the opposite `homOfLE`s is the opposite `homOfLE` of the
551	composite.  Used pervasively to collapse restriction chains in `ses_cech_h1`. -/
552	private lemma restr_trans (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A B C : Opens ↥X}
553	    (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (x : ToType (P.obj (op C))) :
554	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
555	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
556	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
557	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
558	  rfl
559	
560	/-- **Restriction between two equal opens is injective.**  When `V ≤ W` and `W ≤ V`
561	(i.e. `V = W`) the restriction `P(W) → P(V)` is injective: the reverse restriction is a
562	two-sided inverse (`restr_trans` collapses the round trip to the identity, by uniqueness of
563	poset morphisms).  Used to transport the {\v C}ech compatibility equation between the binary
564	intersection `U i ⊓ U j` and the {\v C}ech double-overlap `⨅ₖ U (![i,j] k)`. -/
565	private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
566	    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
567	    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
568	  intro a b hab
569	  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
570	  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
571	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
572	    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2
573	
574	/-- **Two parallel restrictions agree.**  Any two restriction maps `P(A) → P(C)` (opposite
575	opens morphisms) act identically, by uniqueness of poset morphisms.  Used to discharge the
576	final defeq-up-to-proof-irrelevance step after collapsing restriction chains with
577	`← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp`. -/
578	private lemma restr_op_unique (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A C : Opens ↥X}
579	    (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A) (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
580	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
581	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
582	
583	/-- **Restriction of a single-index {\v C}ech section is invariant under reindexing the
584	tuple.**  For two equal `Fin 1`-tuples `ρ = ρ'` (e.g. `fun _ => i` and `![i,j] ∘ δ`), the
585	restrictions of `g' ρ` and `g' ρ'` to a common open `W` agree.  Proved by `subst` (so the
586	propositional-but-not-definitional tuple equality is discharged without a heterogeneous
587	rewrite), then `restr_op_unique`.  This is the bridge between the `U`-indexed glue family
588	(`fun _ => i`) and the {\v C}ech double-overlap agreement (`![i,j] ∘ δ`). -/
589	private lemma restr_g'_transport {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (G : X.PresheafOfModules)
590	    (g' : ∀ ρ : Fin 1 → ι, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))))
591	    {W : Opens ↥X} {ρ ρ' : Fin 1 → ι} (hρ : ρ = ρ')
592	    (f : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W)
593	    (f' : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ' k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W) :
594	    ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f) (g' ρ)
595	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f') (g' ρ') := by
596	  subst hρ
597	  exact restr_op_unique G.presheaf f f' (g' ρ)
598	
599	/-- **A morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with the {\v C}ech face restrictions.**
600	For `fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf` the section-level map `fι.app` intertwines the
601	{\v C}ech face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr` of `F` with that of `G`; this is just
602	the naturality of `fι`.  It is the single naturality input that pushes the cocycle and
603	coboundary identities of the heart between `F` and `G`. -/
604	private lemma fι_sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
605	    (F G : X.PresheafOfModules) (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf)
606	    {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
607	    (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj
608	      (op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l))))) :
609	    ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
610	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) x)
611	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _)
612	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i) x) := by
613	  unfold sectionCechFaceRestr
614	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, fι.naturality]
615	
616	/-- The single-index {\v C}ech intersection at a constant tuple is the cover member. -/
617	private lemma coverConst_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i : ι) :
618	    (⨅ k : Fin 1, U ((fun _ => i) k)) = U i := by simp
619	
620	/-- The double-index {\v C}ech intersection at `![i, j]` is the binary intersection. -/
621	private lemma coverPair_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i j : ι) :
622	    (⨅ k : Fin 2, U (![i, j] k)) = U i ⊓ U j := by
623	  apply le_antisymm
624	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (iInf_le _ 1)
625	  · exact le_iInf (fun k => by fin_cases k <;> simp)
626	
627	/-- The `0`-th face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `j`. -/
628	private lemma pair_comp_δ0 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
629	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (0 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => j) := by
630	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]
631	
632	/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
633	private lemma pair_comp_δ1 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
634	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
635	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]

637	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
638	/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
639	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
640	
641	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
642	— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
643	on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
644	of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
645	`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
646	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.
647	
648	The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
649	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
650	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
651	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
652	section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
653	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
654	single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.
655	
656	Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
657	new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
658	theorem ses_cech_h1 {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
659	    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
660	    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
661	    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
662	    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
663	    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
664	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
665	    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
666	    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
667	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
668	    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
669	    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
670	    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
671	    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
672	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
673	    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
674	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
675	  classical
676	  -- single-index local sections, recast in ⨅-coordinates
677	  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
678	    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
679	    with hsLoc'coord
680	  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
681	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
682	  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
683	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
684	  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
685	  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
686	    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
687	  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
688	    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
689	  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
690	      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
691	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
692	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
693	    intro σ
694	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ
695	    rw [hpe0] at happ
696	    exact happ
697	  -- `gπ` carries a single ⨅-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
698	  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
699	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
700	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
701	    intro ρ h
702	    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
703	      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
704	    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
705	  -- `gπ` kills the differences
706	  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
707	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
708	    intro σ
709	    rw [hdG, map_sum]
710	    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
711	        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
712	          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
713	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
714	        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
715	            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (σ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (σ 0)))).op) s := by
716	      intro i
717	      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
718	      rw [← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gπ σ i,
719	        hgπsLoc' (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]
720	      simp only [sectionCechFaceRestr, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
721	      exact restr_op_unique H.presheaf _ _ s
722	    rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => key i), ← Finset.sum_smul,
723	      show (∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ)) = 0 by decide, zero_smul]
724	  -- lift each difference into `F`
725	  have hc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∃ y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))),
726	      ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) y = dGcoord σ :=
727	    fun σ => hker _ _ (hgπdG σ)
728	  choose c hcspec using hc
729	  -- the `G`-side cocycle identity (`d² = 0`)
730	  have hGcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
731	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
732	        (dGcoord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
733	    intro σ
734	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 1 cyc1 σ
735	    simp only [hdGcoord]
736	    rw [← happ]
737	    have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
738	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 1) cyc1 = 0 := by
739	      rw [hcyc1, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared]
740	      simp
741	    rw [hzero, sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _
742	  -- the `F`-side cocycle identity for the lifted differences
743	  have hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
744	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
745	        (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
746	    intro σ
747	    apply hmono (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))
748	    rw [map_sum, map_zero]
749	    rw [show (0 : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) = _ from (hGcoc σ).symm]
750	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
751	    rw [map_zsmul, ← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i, hcspec]
752	  -- coboundary extraction (the {\v C}ech-`H¹` heart)
753	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology U F hH1 c hcoc
754	  -- the corrected family in ⨅-coordinates
755	  set g'coord : (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) :=
756	    fun ρ => sLoc'coord ρ - ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) (t ρ) with hg'coord
757	  have hg'coc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
758	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
759	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
760	    intro σ
761	    simp only [hg'coord, map_sub, smul_sub, Finset.sum_sub_distrib, ← hdG]
762	    rw [← hcspec σ, ht σ, map_sum, sub_eq_zero]
763	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
764	    rw [map_zsmul, ← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i]
765	  -- pairwise agreement of the corrected family on double overlaps
766	  have hagree2 : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
767	      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 0)
768	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 0).toOrderHom))
769	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 1)
770	        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 1).toOrderHom)) := by
771	    intro σ
772	    have h := hg'coc σ
773	    rw [Fin.sum_univ_two] at h
774	    simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, one_smul, pow_one, neg_one_zsmul] at h
775	    rwa [add_neg_eq_zero] at h
776	  -- the `U`-indexed corrected family
777	  set gGlue : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
778	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom
779	      (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_of_eq (coverConst_iInf U i).symm)).op) (g'coord (fun _ => i))
780	    with hgGlue
781	  have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf U gGlue := by
782	    intro i j
783	    apply restr_inj_of_eq G.presheaf (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j))
784	      (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j).symm)
785	    simp only [hgGlue]
786	    erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
787	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← G.presheaf.map_comp,
788	      ← G.presheaf.map_comp, ← G.presheaf.map_comp, ← G.presheaf.map_comp]
789	    have key := hagree2 ![i, j]
790	    simp only [sectionCechFaceRestr] at key
791	    exact (restr_g'_transport U G g'coord (pair_comp_δ1 i j).symm _ _).trans
792	      (key.symm.trans (restr_g'_transport U G g'coord (pair_comp_δ0 i j).symm _ _).symm)
793	  -- glue the corrected family to a global section of `G`
794	  obtain ⟨g, hgluing, -⟩ := hGsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U gGlue hcompat
795	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
796	  -- `gπ g = s` by separatedness of `H`
797	  set sH : ∀ i, ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
798	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s with hsH
799	  have hcompatH : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH := by
800	    intro i j
801	    simp only [hsH]
802	    erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← H.presheaf.map_comp,
803	      ← H.presheaf.map_comp]
804	    exact restr_op_unique H.presheaf _ _ s
805	  have hg2 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH s := fun i => rfl
806	  have hgπglue : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i) = sH i := by
807	    intro i
808	    simp only [hgGlue, hsH, hg'coord]
809	    rw [show Opens.leSupr U i = homOfLE (le_iSup U i) from rfl,
810	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, map_sub,
811	      hπι, sub_zero, hgπsLoc' _ ((coverConst_iInf U i).le.trans (le_iSup U i)), restr_trans]
812	  have hg1 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH
813	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) := by
814	    intro i
815	    have hn : ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
816	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g)
817	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i) := by
818	      rw [← hgluing i]
819	      erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← gπ.naturality (Opens.leSupr U i).op,
820	        ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
821	    show ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
822	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) = sH i
823	    rw [hn]
824	    exact hgπglue i
825	  exact (hHsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U sH hcompatH).unique hg1 hg2
826	
827	end SesCechH1

8	/-!
9	# Higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` of quasi-coherent sheaves (`i ≥ 1`)
10	
11	This file treats the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`,
12	complementing the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`.
13	
14	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of
15	modules on `X`. The `i`-th higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the `i`-th right
16	derived functor of the pushforward functor `f_*` applied to `F`.
17	
18	This file supplies the single declaration needed downstream by the Čech
19	computation of higher direct images:
20	
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage` — the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F`,
22	  the derived-functor object that `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` compares the
23	  Čech complex against.
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex`.
26	
27	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Definition of `Rⁱ f_*`.
28	-/
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits
33	
34	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
35	
36	open Scheme.Modules
37

48	    (f : X ⟶ S) (i : ℕ) (F : X.Modules) : S.Modules :=
49	  ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F
